title: 'Cover-up' concealment narrative
summary: A delay posture under rising media pressure let a concealment frame take hold.
escalation_note: The concealment frame hardened another cycle without a full public accounting.
resolution_note: A full public accounting broke the concealment frame.

title: State oversight designation threat
summary: Late notification and sliding metrics put a formal oversight review in play.
escalation_note: The oversight review file thickened while local metrics kept sliding.
resolution_note: Oversight risk fell back below the designation threshold.

title: Sole-source contractor precedent
summary: Conceded emergency terms are hardening into structural dependency.
escalation_note: The sole-source emergency terms were cited as precedent again; procurement-law exposure and state attention both deepened.
resolution_note: Dependency fell far enough to renegotiate on normal terms.

title: School closure standoff
summary: Parents and the superintendent demand an on-the-record closure threshold.
escalation_note: Another cycle without a published closure threshold kept parents improvising and the superintendent exposed.
resolution_note: A published closure protocol ended the standoff.

title: Public-trust collapse
summary: Trust has fallen far enough that emergency measures lose public consent.
escalation_note: With consent eroded, compliance with emergency measures slipped further.
resolution_note: Trust recovered enough for emergency measures to hold.

title: Grid margin erosion
summary: Peak load is eating the substation margin; without load management the desk's own infrastructure starts to brown out.
escalation_note: Another peak-load cycle passed without a load-management order; feeder trips are getting longer.
resolution_note: Load management restored a defensible operating margin.
